name: Uruguay
avoid: January is peak beach season, so Punta del Este and Cabo Polonio prices spike fast.
Winter (June-August) is quieter but windy, and many coastal businesses reduce hours.
stopoverNote: Pairs naturally with Buenos Aires via the Buquebus ferry to Colonia del Sacramento.

cities.name: Montevideo
cities.notes: Historic Ciudad Vieja, long Rambla sunsets, parrillas, and Uruguay's most useful transit hub.

cities.name: Colonia del Sacramento
cities.notes: Cobblestone UNESCO quarter with river views and the easiest ferry link from Buenos Aires.

cities.name: Punta del Este
cities.notes: Classic South Atlantic resort base for beaches, port strolls, and the Casapueblo sunset.

cities.name: Cabo Polonio
cities.notes: Remote dune-backed fishing hamlet with 4x4-only access, a lighthouse, and sea lion colony.

cities.name: Salto
cities.notes: Thermal-springs stop in northwest Uruguay, best for a slower inland break after the coast.

itinerary.sim: Antel prepago is the easiest local option; expect about ₹700–₹1,400 for a useful tourist-sized data pack. Coverage is solid in Montevideo, Colonia, Punta del Este, and Salto, but download maps before Cabo Polonio because signal can be patchy inside the dunes village.

Colonia del Sacramento.name: Colonia del Sacramento
Colonia del Sacramento.note: This is Uruguay's easiest soft landing from Buenos Aires. Stay inside or beside the Barrio Histórico so you can walk the quarter before and after day-trippers thin out.

activities.name: Walk the Barrio Histórico lanes around Calle de los Suspiros, Portón de Campo, and Plaza Mayor
Faro de Colonia climb plus the small municipal museum circuit
Basílica del Santísimo Sacramento and Bastión de San Miguel viewpoints
Sunset along the old city wall and Rambla de Colonia

Montevideo.name: Montevideo
Montevideo.note: Split your time between Ciudad Vieja and the east-side waterfront districts. Mercado del Puerto is best for lunch; evenings are ideal for the Rambla and Pocitos.

days.theme: Ciudad Vieja, Plaza Independencia & Mercado del Puerto

activities.name: Mercado del Puerto food-hall stroll and grill lunch at the old iron market
Plaza Independencia, Puerta de la Ciudadela, and Palacio Salvo exterior walk
Teatro Solís guided visit
Museo Andes 1972

days.theme: Rambla, Parque Rodó & Carrasco-side Food and Wine

activities.name: Morning walk through Parque Rodó and the Rambla from Punta Carretas toward Pocitos
Museo Nacional de Artes Visuales
Bodega Bouza winery visit with tasting or lunch
Sunset at Carrasco beach or Plaza Virgilio lookout

Punta del Este.name: Punta del Este
Punta del Este.note: Base near Playa Mansa or the peninsula if you want easy access to the port, beaches, and Punta Ballena for Casapueblo at sunset.

activities.name: La Mano sculpture, Playa Brava photo stop, and peninsula walk
Puerto de Punta del Este marina and sea lion lookout
Casapueblo museum visit in Punta Ballena
Sunset ceremony at Casapueblo terrace

hotels.name: The Grand Hotel Punta del Este

days.theme: Beach Day, Isla Gorriti or Museum Option

activities.name: Playa Mansa or Bikini Beach morning depending on your base
Boat out to Isla Gorriti from the port when the weather is calm
Museo Ralli as the art-focused backup if beach weather turns
Evening shopping and café stop around Avenida Gorlero

Cabo Polonio.name: Cabo Polonio
Cabo Polonio.note: Travel light because access is by official park truck over sand. Book simple lodging ahead in summer and carry cash plus a power bank because services are basic.

activities.name: Official 4x4 truck transfer between the national-park access gate and the village
Cabo Polonio lighthouse climb and viewpoint
Walk the giant dunes and the sea lion colony on the rocky coast
Beach time at Playa Sur before the last truck back or an overnight stay

Salto.name: Salto
Salto.note: Salto works best as a slow thermal reset. Stay by Daymán if hot springs are your priority, or in town if you want restaurants and easy riverfront access.

days.theme: Termas del Daymán & Thermal Park Relaxation

activities.name: Termas del Daymán thermal complex day pass
Acuamanía water park if you want slides instead of a pure spa day
Evening soak and casual riverside dinner
Parque del Lago and Salto Grande dam viewpoint
Museo del Hombre y la Tecnología
Costanera Norte walk for river views and mate culture
Optional second thermal stop at Daymán or Arapey depending on your transport

connections.method: COT intercity bus takes about 2 hr 30 min; rental car is similarly quick if you want flexibility
COPSA intercity bus from Tres Cruces is the standard 2–2.5 hr coastal hop; rental car is easy on Ruta Interbalnearia
Take a COPSA or COT-style coastal bus toward Castillos/Valizas access, then the official park truck into Cabo Polonio
Rental car is the cleanest same-day move at roughly 7–8 hrs; by public transport expect the park truck out plus long intercity bus connections via the coast or Montevideo
Buquebus ferry is the classic Río de la Plata crossing and makes Colonia the easiest international entry point

extras: December to March is the sweet spot for beaches and long daylight, but book Punta del Este and Cabo Polonio beds early for late December through January.
Mercado del Puerto is best treated as a lunch stop rather than a quick snack break because tables and parrillas are the whole point of the visit.
If you are short on time, keep Colonia, Montevideo, and Punta del Este as the core route and treat Cabo Polonio plus Salto as rewarding extensions.
COPSA and COT cover the most useful tourist corridors, but a rental car becomes much more convenient once you add Cabo Polonio access and inland Salto to the same trip.
Carry some cash for rural stops and simple beach lodgings even though cards are widely accepted in Montevideo, Colonia, and Punta del Este.

Colonia del Sacramento: Colonia del Sacramento Calle de los Suspiros
Punta del Este: Punta del Este port sea lions
